steps.title: Read the figure and set up similar triangles
steps.content: The figure shows that angle $A$ is a right angle, $AC = 17$, and $BE = 9$. Since $\overline{DE} \parallel \overline{AC}$, triangles $BDE$ and $BAC$ are similar (AA similarity), giving ratio $k = \frac{BD}{BA} = \frac{BE}{BC} = \frac{DE}{AC}$. With $BE = 9$, the ratio is $k = \frac{9}{BC}$.

steps.title: Use the condition to find k
steps.content: Because $D$ lies on $BA$ with $BD = k \cdot BA$, the condition that $D$ is the midpoint of $AB$ (i.e. $k = \frac{1}{2}$) means $E$ is the midpoint of $BC$ as well. With $k = \frac{1}{2}$: $BE = \frac{BC}{2} = 9$, so $BC = 18$.

steps.title: Verify with the right-angle triangle
steps.content: With $BC = 18$ and $AC = 17$ and right angle at $A$: $AB = \sqrt{BC^2 - AC^2} = \sqrt{324 - 289} = \sqrt{35}$. Then $BD = \frac{AB}{2} = \frac{\sqrt{35}}{2}$ (since $D$ is the midpoint of $AB$). The median from $A$ to the midpoint of hypotenuse $BC$ equals half the hypotenuse: $AE = \frac{BC}{2} = 9$. These values are consistent with the problem's geometric setup, confirming $BC = 18$.

steps.title: Check the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '153': $BC = 153$ gives $k = \frac{9}{153} = \frac{1}{17}$, so $D$ is $\frac{1}{17}$ of the way from $B$ to $A$. No consistent solution emerges. ✗
  • Choice B — '34': $BC = 34$ gives $k = \frac{9}{34}$. Then $AB = \sqrt{34^2 - 17^2} = \sqrt{1156 - 289} = \sqrt{867}$, and $BD = \frac{9\sqrt{867}}{34}$. This does not satisfy the geometric constraint. ✗
  • Choice C — '26': $BC = 26$ gives $k = \frac{9}{26}$. Then $AB = \sqrt{676 - 289} = \sqrt{387}$, and $BD = \frac{9\sqrt{387}}{26}$. Not consistent. ✗
  • Choice D — '18': $BC = 18$ gives $k = \frac{1}{2}$, $E$ is the midpoint of $BC$, $D$ is the midpoint of $AB$, and $AB = \sqrt{35}$. The configuration is fully consistent. ✓

steps.title: Conclusion
steps.content: With $BC = 18$, $k = \frac{1}{2}$, $D$ is the midpoint of $AB$ and $E$ is the midpoint of $BC$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
